pattern: First-come-first-served matching over two independent arrival queues reduces to peeling off the front of each queue, except a cancelled rider must be skipped rather than matched — so the rider side needs a cheap way to tell a still-waiting id from one that already left the line.
hints: Riders and drivers each arrive in their own order and should be matched oldest-first from each side, independent of the other side's arrival times.

A cancelled rider is still physically at its old position in line; rather than removing it from the middle of a queue, mark it cancelled and have matching skip over any cancelled id it finds at the front.

Keep two arrival-ordered queues (riders, drivers) plus a set of rider ids that are still actually waiting; before matching, drop from the front of the rider queue anything no longer in that waiting set, then take the front of both queues.

edge_inputs.description: sample 1: a match, a cancel on an already-matched rider (no effect), then an empty match
sample 2: a match, then a cancelled waiting rider leaves nothing to match
cancelling a rider that is not at the front of the queue must still be skipped correctly when its turn would have come up
no riders waiting at all means a match cannot be made even with drivers available
no drivers waiting at all means a match cannot be made even with riders available
cancelling a riderId that was never added is a harmless no-op
riderId and driverId at the constraint extremes
drivers and riders are each matched strictly in their own arrival order, not by id order

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['RideSharingSystem']
    args = [[]]
    next_rider = [1]
    next_driver = [1]
    waiting_riders = []
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.35:
            rid = next_rider[0]
            next_rider[0] += 1
            ops.append('addRider')
            args.append([rid])
            waiting_riders.append(rid)
        elif r < 0.6:
            did = next_driver[0]
            next_driver[0] += 1
            ops.append('addDriver')
            args.append([did])
        elif r < 0.8 and waiting_riders:
            rid = rng.choice(waiting_riders)
            waiting_riders.remove(rid)
            ops.append('cancelRider')
            args.append([rid])
        else:
            ops.append('matchDriverWithRider')
            args.append([])
            if waiting_riders:
                waiting_riders.pop(0)
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: ids increase monotonically per role so no rider or driver id is ever reused; the generator's own waiting_riders list stays a loose upper bound on who could still be matched
